2606.23233.title: Judgment-Grounded Expansion for Peer Review Generation
2606.23233.paper_summary: Automatic review generation is a promising direction for accelerating scientific progress. While most work adopts an end-to-end setup, its fully automated nature makes it less suitable for settings that demand accountability. To better balance automation and accountability, we formalize judgment-grounded expansion, a human-AI collaboration mode where a reviewer provides an evaluative claim and the system expands it into review comment candidate(s). We model it as a structured generate-check-refine process and conduct a user study to collect human-model interaction data. We study two practical challenges for judgment-grounded expansion: scalable evaluation and candidate set curation. We develop methods to simulate the process for large-scale evaluation, and show that conformal prediction is well suited to balancing candidate set size and target coverage. Our work establishes judgment-grounded expansion as a concrete task and provides empirical and methodological foundations for the design of future collaborative review generation systems.
2606.23233.paper_summary_zh: 自動評審生成是一個有前景的方向，可以加速科學進步。雖然大多數工作採用端到端的設置，但其完全自動化的特性使其不太適合需要問責的環境。為了更好地平衡自動化和問責，我們正式化了基於判斷的擴展，這是一種人機協作模式，其中審稿人提供評估性主張，系統將其擴展為評審意見候選項。我們將其建模為一個結構化的生成-檢查-精煉過程，並進行用戶研究以收集人類與模型的互動數據。我們研究了基於判斷的擴展的兩個實際挑戰：可擴展的評估和候選集的策劃。我們開發了模擬大規模評估過程的方法，並展示了保形預測非常適合平衡候選集的大小和目標覆蓋。我們的工作將基於判斷的擴展建立為一個具體任務，並為未來協作評審生成系統的設計提供了實證和方法論基礎。
2606.23233.author: Sheng Lu et.al.
2606.23233.authors: Sheng Lu, Lizhen Qu, Iryna Gurevych
